title: Be Anxious for Nothing
scripture_text: Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
body: Worry tells us to carry what was never ours to hold. Paul gives a better way: bring it to God in prayer, with thanksgiving, and let His peace stand guard over your heart. Today, name the thing you are anxious about and hand it to Him on purpose.
closing_prayer: Father, I bring my worries to You today. Trade my anxiety for Your peace, and guard my heart in Christ. Amen.
reflection_questions: What are you anxious about right now?|What would it look like to thank God in the middle of it?

title: Strength in the Waiting
scripture_text: But they that wait upon the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles.
body: Waiting is not wasted. God uses the wait to renew what striving drained. If you are tired today, the answer is not to push harder but to wait on Him and let Him lift you.
closing_prayer: Lord, renew my strength as I wait on You. Lift me above what is weighing me down. Amen.
reflection_questions: Where do you feel worn out today?|How can you 'wait on the Lord' this week instead of striving?

title: A Lamp to My Feet
scripture_text: Thy word is a lamp unto my feet, and a light unto my path.
body: God rarely lights the whole road at once. He gives enough light for the next step. Open the Word today and take the step you can see.
closing_prayer: Father, light my next step through Your Word. Give me courage to walk in what I already know. Amen.
reflection_questions: What decision are you waiting on God for?|What is the next faithful step you can take today?

title: Cast Your Cares
scripture_text: Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.
body: You were not made to be the one who holds it all together. Peter says cast your care on Him, because He cares for you. Not just the big things. All of it.
closing_prayer: Lord, I cast my cares on You. Thank You that You care about every detail of my life. Amen.
reflection_questions: What 'care' have you been carrying that you can give to God today?
